nodes.title: Split className into stable tokens
nodes.why: Tokenizing once avoids class matching bugs caused by extra whitespace.
nodes.alternative: Use raw string checks like className.includes().
nodes.tradeoff: Small parsing cost, but correct class matching behavior.

nodes.title: Parse #id and .class with explicit fast paths
nodes.why: Direct branches make selector intent easy to follow.
nodes.alternative: Use one generic parser for all selector forms.
nodes.tradeoff: More branches, but easier to debug and extend.

nodes.title: Normalize selector whitespace before parsing chain
nodes.why: Users may pass extra spaces; this keeps selector behavior consistent.
nodes.alternative: Trust input formatting and split on a single space.
nodes.tradeoff: One normalization step, but fewer edge-case failures.

nodes.title: Match current node first, then walk ancestors backward
nodes.why: Descendant chains naturally validate from the last token back to the first.
nodes.alternative: Search from root downward for every token.
nodes.tradeoff: Ancestor walk per candidate can cost more, but logic is straightforward.

nodes.title: DFS visits child elements in document order
nodes.why: This preserves the expected querySelectorAll output order.
nodes.alternative: Collect nodes first, then sort later.
nodes.tradeoff: Single pass is cleaner; sorting would add extra overhead.

nodes.title: Check root as a candidate before walking descendants
nodes.why: query-like behavior should include root when it matches.
nodes.alternative: Only test descendants and skip root.
nodes.tradeoff: One extra check, but complete and expected results.
